title: 10 best herbal essential oils for overactive bladder
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for overactive bladder are natural, plant-based extracts that can provide relief from urinary frequency, urgency, and incontinence. These oils work by calming the muscles of the bladder and urethra, reducing spasms and inflammation, and promoting relaxation. Examples of herbal essential oils that help with overactive bladder include lavender, chamomile, and juniper berry. By incorporating these oils into daily life through aromatherapy or topical application, individuals can experience improved sleep quality, increased confidence, and a reduced sense of anxiety and embarrassment associated with urinary incontinence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry essential oils helps with overactive bladder because it contains compounds that have natural antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The oil's active ingredients, such as arbutin and hydroquinone, can help reduce the frequency of urination by soothing the urinary tract and reducing bacteria growth. Additionally, bearberry oil has been shown to strengthen the bladder muscles, allowing for more efficient urine storage and reduced symptoms of overactive bladder, leading to improved overall bladder function and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Uricosuric: Bearberry essential oil has uricosuric properties, which means it helps to reduce the production of excess urate in the body, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of bearberry essential oil may help to soothe and calm irritated tissues in the urinary tract, reducing discomfort and pain associated with overactive bladder.

Antimicrobial: Bearberry essential oil has antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bearberry plant contain a high concentration of anthraquinones, which have been shown to help relax the muscles in the urinary tract and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.

Barks: The barks of the bearberry plant are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems in early morning or late afternoon when fragrant oils are highest.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or until completely dry.
Steam distill dried arctostaphylos uva ursi material with distilled water in a clevenger apparatus at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil using a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the obtained essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature, away from direct sunlight for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry essential oils helps with overactive bladder because of its unique composition, rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, which have been shown to possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can contribute to frequent urination and urgency. Additionally, highbush cranberry essential oils may also help to strengthen the pelvic muscles, improving bladder control and overall urinary function.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Highbush cranberry essential oil has antispasmodic properties, which means it can help relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra to reduce frequency and urgency of urination.

Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe and calm the inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms, reducing discomfort and improving overall bladder health.

Antimicrobial: Highbush cranberry essential oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to overactive bladder symptoms, such as urinary tract infections (UTIs).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the highbush cranberry plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Barks: The barks of the highbush cranberry plant contain a unique combination of tannins and saponins that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urethra, providing relief from frequent urination and urgency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vaccinium macrocarpon berries fresh from the wild or cultivate them in a controlled environment.
Dry the harvested berries in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Use a mechanical press to extract the essential oil from dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries at a ratio of 1 part berries to 3 parts carrier oil.
Distill the extracted oil using steam distillation method for 2 hours, collecting the essential oil in a separate container.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow essential oils helps with overactive bladder because of its soothing and calming properties. The oil contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats the urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and irritation. This can help to calm the bladder muscles, reducing spasms and frequency of urination. Additionally, marshmallow oil has natural antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the bladder and pelvic floor muscles, providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms such as urgency, frequency, and incontinence.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, which may contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Soothing and calming properties: The essential oil has a soothing and calming effect on the mucous membranes, which can help relieve discomfort and burning sensations associated with overactive bladder.

Antispasmodic properties: Marshmallow root essential oil contains compounds that can relax smooth muscle spasms in the urinary tract, reducing frequency of urination and alleviating symptoms of urgency and frequency.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a soothing and calming essential oil that helps to reduce urinary frequency and urgency.
Barks: The barks contain mucilage, which helps to coat and protect the bladder lining, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marsof (althaea officinalis) leaves in the early morning, choosing those with no signs of pests or diseases.
Wash the harvested leaves with distilled water and gently pat dry using a clean towel to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill 100g of dried marsof leaves in a 500ml capacity still for 90 minutes at a temperature of 100°c, collecting the resulting essential oil.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a paper coffee filter or a glass filter into a sterile dark glass bottle, discarding any impurities and sediment.
Store the herbal althaea officinalis essential oil in a cool, dark place for a minimum of 2 weeks before using it to allow maturation and stabilization.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with overactive bladder because it has a calming effect on the urinary tract, reducing spasms and frequency. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe the bladder lining, relieving irritation and discomfort. Additionally, sage oil's antispasmodic properties can help to relax the muscles in the bladder, allowing for more efficient emptying and reducing the urge to urinate frequently. This natural remedy provides relief from the constant trips to the bathroom and promotes overall urinary tract health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Herbal sage essential oil can help relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with overactive bladder. This can lead to a decrease in frequency and urgency of urination.

Astringent: The astringent properties of herbal sage essential oil may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, which can exacerbate symptoms of overactive bladder. By soothing the mucous membranes, it can help calm the urge to urinate.

Antioxidant: Herbal sage essential oil's antioxidant properties may help protect the tissues of the urinary tract from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and bladder dysfunction.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and inflammation, making them a suitable choice for addressing overactive bladder symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of sage contains bioactive compounds like camphor and borneol, which can help soothe and calm the urinary tract muscles, reducing frequency and urgency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves in late summer or early fall at dawn when the plant is fully grown.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 100g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 200ml of high-proof vodka and store mixture in a dark glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking daily, then filter out plant material through cheesecloth.
Transfer the resulting liquid to a still or steam distillation apparatus and collect essential oil as it condenses.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with overactive bladder because of its natural soothing properties that calm the nervous system, reducing muscle spasms and contractions that can lead to frequent urination. The oil's ability to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract also alleviates symptoms such as urgency and frequency. Additionally, lemon balm has antispasmodic effects, which help regulate bladder function and ease discomfort associated with overactive bladder syndrome.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Herbal lemon balm essential oil helps to relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing spasms and contractions that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms and improve overall bladder health.

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the bladder lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation that may worsen overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant have a calming effect, which can help soothe an overactive bladder and reduce urinary frequency.
Barks: The bark of the lemon balm plant is rich in flavonoids, which can help relax muscles and reduce spasms, making it effective for treating urinary incontinence.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried off.
Dry the harvested plant material using a warm air circulation dryer for 24 hours at 60-70°f temperature.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis plant material in a stainless steel still with 1 liter of water at 212°f pressure.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the distilled water using a condenser coil and filter paper.
Bottle and store the extracted melissa officinalis essential oil in dark glass containers for up to 6 months without refrigeration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree essential oils helps with overactive bladder because it has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to frequent urination. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate discomfort and pressure in the pelvic area, while its natural antispasmodic effects can help relax muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing urgency and frequency of bathroom visits. As a result, chaste tree essential oils may provide relief for individuals suffering from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: This property helps to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can contribute to frequent urination and urgency.
Anti-inflammatory property: This property helps to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as burning sensations during urination.

Uterorelaxant property: This property helps to relax the uterine muscles, which can also help to alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder by reducing muscle spasms and contractions that can lead to frequent urination.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in phytochemicals, roots are used to create a soothing and calming oil that helps to relax the urinary tract muscles, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids and phenolic acids, chaste tree flowers yield an essential oil that has been traditionally used to ease bladder irritation and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh vitex agnus castus leaves and flowers on a sunny day for optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested plant material using 3 cups of water at 100°c for 90 minutes to release the essential oil.
Separate the obtained essential oil from the hydrosol by allowing it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.
Filter the solidified essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle for 15 minutes.
Store the prepared vitex agnus castus essential oil in an airtight container in a cool place protected from light.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley essential oils helps with overactive bladder because of its natural ability to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and frequency. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help soothe the bladder lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, parsley essential oil has been shown to stimulate digestion and improve overall gut health, which can also contribute to a reduction in overactive bladder symptoms. By using parsley essential oils topically or aromatically, individuals can experience relief from frequent urination and urinary incontinence.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Parsley oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms by reducing muscle spasms and alleviating discomfort.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of parsley oil help relax smooth muscle tissue in the urinary tract, reducing muscle contractions that can cause frequent urination and incontinence.

Antioxidant property: Parsley oil's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage in the bladder and urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms by reducing inflammation and scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Parsley roots contain compounds like apiol and myristicin, which have been shown to relax the muscles in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Seeds: Parsley seeds contain volatile oils rich in terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe the bladder and urethra.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality petroselinum crispum leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a clevenger apparatus with distilled water at 100°c for 1 hour and collect 10ml of essential oil.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment adding 5 drops of jojoba carrier oil for blending.
Store the petroselinum crispum essential oil blend in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn essential oils helps with overactive bladder because they possess natural properties that calm and soothe the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory compounds in corn oil, such as linoleic acid, work to ease bladder contractions and spasms, allowing for more efficient and controlled urination. Additionally, corn oil's antispasmodic effects help relax the muscles surrounding the bladder, alleviating symptoms of frequent or sudden urges to void. This natural remedy provides relief from the discomfort and anxiety associated with overactive bladder, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Cranberry seed oil: Contains omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants that may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Saw palmetto oil: Has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that may help alleviate urinary frequency and urgency by relaxing the muscles in the bladder and urethra.
Uva ursi oil: Contains compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce bacterial growth in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the corn plant contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the corn plant have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract issues, including incontinence and frequent urination, due to their astringent and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh corn silk or dried corn cob fragments and dry them completely for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried corn silk or corn cob fragments in a still with 30 ml of distilled water at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove any impurities within an hour.
Blend the filtered essential oil with 10 drops of lavender essential oil and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid for up to 6 months.
Use the blended essential oil by adding 2-3 drops to a warm bath or applying topically after diluting with a carrier oil at least 30 minutes before bedtime.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with overactive bladder because they possess a unique combination of compounds that provide relief from urinary incontinence. The oil's natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties help to soothe the bladder muscles, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, its calming effects on the nervous system may also contribute to reduced muscle contractions, leading to improved bladder control and overall management of overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Certain essential oils like peppermint (Mentha piperita) and chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) have antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the urinary tract and reduce spasms that may contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Essential oils like tea tree oil (Melaleuca alternifolia) and lavender oil (Lavandula angustifolia) have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat urinary tract infections (UTIs) that may contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Calming and relaxing properties: Essential oils like bergamot (Citrus aurantium bergamia) and ylang-ylang (Cananga odorata) have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract.
Barks: Contain terpenes and phenolic compounds that can help reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle contractions in the bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from the plant ensuring 10-15% concentration of flavonoids and terpenes.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm ventilated area for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours.
Chop the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar with a lid covering 50% of the jar's capacity.
Infuse the chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a ratio of 1:5 for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Filter the infusion using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store the resulting essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder essential oils helps with overactive bladder because it has a calming effect on the urinary tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can lead to frequent urination. The oil's antispasmodic properties also help to relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, allowing for more efficient storage and elimination of urine. Additionally, elder essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the urinary tract, further alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Elder essential oil helps relax muscle spasms in the urinary tract, reducing frequency and urgency of urination due to its ability to calm and soothe irritated muscles.

Anti-inflammatory: Elder essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms such as burning sensations while urinating and pain during urination.
Antimicrobial: Elder essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help prevent bacterial infections, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms like frequent urination and urgency.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, particularly berberine and protoberberine, which have been shown to help reduce urinary frequency and urgency.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing bladder irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra leaves and flowers on a sunny day for optimal oil quality.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried sambucus nigra leaves and flowers into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Steam distill the crushed plant material in a still with 1 part plant material to 5 parts water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or coffee filters to remove impurities within an hour of distillation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat overactive bladder with herbal essential oils is by combining specific blends of oils that address urinary tract inflammation and relaxation. A soothing blend of lavender, chamomile, and bergamot oils can help calm the bladder muscles, while a stimulating blend of peppermint, ginger, and uva ursi oils can aid in reducing symptoms such as urgency and frequency. Apply these blends topically to the lower abdomen or inhale them through steam inhalation for effective relief.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overactive bladder that are treated with herbal essential oils are urinary incontinence, frequent urination, and nocturia. Essential oils such as lavender, chamomile, and lemon balm have been used to soothe and calm the bladder muscles, reducing symptoms of urgency and frequency. Other herbs like uva ursi and corn silk may also help alleviate urinary tract issues by anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with these conditions.
